title: Teams search retry — SSE first-line filter misses token-refresh responses
description: data-refresh.sh Teams path fails on cases with long-idle agency proxy. mcp_search_with_retry uses `grep -o 'data: {.*}' | sed | head -1` to extract JSON-RPC result; when agency resolves 401 challenge mid-stream, the first SSE frame is an auth keepalive/error event, not the result. `head -1` discards the valid frame. Repro: case 2604140040001804 DELTA_OK smoke → TEAMS_FAIL|reason=all 1 search(es) errored|elapsed=103s, while manual `curl` against fresh proxy returns 6 hits in <2s. Agency log shows: `Upstream returned 401 — resolving auth from challenge` → `Auth token acquired duration_ms=968`. Fix candidates: (1) drop `head -1`, concat all data: frames then pick the one containing `"result"`; (2) warm auth token with a dummy search before the real query (similar to RDS agency pre-warming); (3) bump retry count from 1 to 2 and widen sleep to 10s to give token refresh time. Scope: .claude/skills/teams-search/scripts/teams-search-inline.sh.
